heading: How do I disable peering-only mode?
body:
To disable Peering-Only mode using the:

**Console:**

Atlas Console

Change any URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s in your applications that use your
Atlas clusters to use
[Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).

Toggle Connect via Peering Only (GCP and Azure) to Off.

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

Call the [get all](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint to
return all clusters and their MongoDB versions:

> **Note:** This `curl` command uses a service account access token ([OAuth 2.0](https://oauth.net/2/)) to authenticate
> instead of API keys. To learn more, see [API Access Lists endpoint](atlas-admin-api-access).

> **Example:** 
>
> ```sh
> curl --header "Authorization: Bearer {ACCESS-TOKEN}" \
>      --header "Accept: application/json" \
>      --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
>      --include \
>      --request GET "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v1.0/groups/{GROUP-ID}/clusters?pretty=true"
> ```
>
> If successful, the response should include:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "results": [{
>     ...
>     "mongoDBMajorVersion": "6.0",
>     "mongoDBVersion": "6.0.14",
>     ...
>   },{
>     ...
>     "mongoDBMajorVersion": "6.0",
>     "mongoDBVersion": "6.0.12",
>     ...       }
>   ]
> }
> ```

Change any URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s in your applications that use your
Atlas clusters to use
[Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).

Call the API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint to [Disable Private IP Mode](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

> **Example:** Using `curl`, you would invoke this command:
>
> ```sh
> curl --header "Authorization: Bearer {ACCESS-TOKEN}" \
>      --header "Accept: application/json" \
>      --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
>      --include \
>      --request PATCH "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v1.0/groups/{GROUP-ID}/privateIpMode?pretty=true" \
>      --data '
>        {
>          "enabled" : false
>        }'
> ```
>
> Change `{GROUP-ID}` to the
> [maintenance](atlas-modify-project-settings).
>
> If successful, the response displays:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "enabled" : false
> }
> ```

codeBlocks.content:
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer {ACCESS-TOKEN}" \
     --header "Accept: application/json" \
     --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
     --include \
     --request PATCH "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v1.0/groups/{GROUP-ID}/privateIpMode?pretty=true" \
     --data '
       {
         "enabled" : false
       }'
